7. Tech Envo Migration Framework  – SAP SuccessFactors Integration Approach

As part of the migration, 

We develop high-quality integrations in SAP Integration Suite, utilizing its full capabilities to ensure optimal performance. Our approach prioritizes leveraging pre-delivered features with minimal customization by using standard mapping functions and predefined process steps offered by SAP Integration Suite. We also enforce industry-leading development standards aligned with SAP best practices and organizations' leading practices, ensuring consistency, maintainability, and scalability across all integrations.

 

  1. We implement direct API-based Direct integrations if the vendor has built integration and is available in the SAP App Center (e.g., Hire Right, Nakisa, JDMS, SailPoint, Omada IDM, Okta, etc.). Direct integrations avoid the middleware layer and reduce development and support costs.
  2. We implement prepackaged integrations developed by SAP, Tech Envo, or Vendor to reduce development effort and customization.
  3.  We use SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center (IC) if the Integration Center integrations require less effort to build and maintain.
  4. We have built reusable integration templates, which we can use if we need to develop a custom integration. 

 

8. SAP Integration Suite Technical Landscape, Cloud Foundry Environment

  • Cloud Foundry: The Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP is an open-source application runtime supporting Java, Node.js, and other languages, ideal for extending SAP products, integrating applications, and building enterprise solutions using SAP-hosted APIs. 
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/technical-landscape-cloud-foundry-environment 

 

9. Deployment Environments of SAP Integration Suite

  • Cloud Integration Runtime: A cloud-based service in SAP Integration Suite, enabling seamless data exchange between cloud and on-premise applications like Boomi Cloud  Atoms.
  • Edge Integration Cell: A hybrid integration runtime in SAP Integration Suite that allows designing in the cloud while running integrations on-premise, equivalent to Boomi On-Premise Atom
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/setting-up-and-managing-edge-integration-cell 
  • Hybrid Deployment Option: Customer can use both Cloud Integration Runtime and Edge Integration Cell - Hybrid deployment option. 

 

10. SFEC to SAP ERP/S4HANA Integrations Migration Approach

We migrate Boomi legacy SAP S4 HANA, SAP ERP HCM, SAP ECC integrations to the BIB Replication Framework for seamless. Employee and Organizational Data replication. Our expertise includes implementing BIB-based integrations for multiple customers, including SAP S/4HANA environments. We have developed accelerators to handle complex replication scenarios, such as dynamic actions, pensioners’ data replication, concurrent employment, and global assignments, ensuring a robust and efficient migration process and no impact to payroll during migration.

 

Migration Key Considerations

1.Connectivity

  • The IP address changes for  Boomi data center (Atoms) and SAP IS are different, so the firewall ports and network config must be updated with new IP range details during migration.
  • Server and Client certificates in the on-premise ERP system must also be updated during migration.
  • Any custom integrations built using SuccessFactors SMTP server for sending emails must be changed to a publicly available SMTP server, as SuccessFactors does not allow external access to its SMTP server.
  • Any locally installed atom needs to be changed to use SAP Cloud Connector (SCC)/Edge Integration. SAP Cloud Connector connects on-premise systems with SAP IS without opening tunnels.

2.Instance Strategy:

  • Depending on the license model, SAP provides two tenants (Test and Production) by default, but we recommend using a three-tier architecture.
  • Multi-tier architecture requires an additional SAP IS tenant, which may involve extra costs.

 

3.Cloud to On-Premise Connectivity

  • On-premise connections via Boomi Local Atom need migration to SAP Cloud Connector or Edge Integration Cell.
  • Proxy tools used in Boomi can still be leveraged in SAP Cloud Integration.

 

4.Interface Mapping: Existing interfaces need to be evaluated based on the following 4 buckets:

  1. Boomi Pre-Packaged to SAP IS Pre-Packaged /Direct API-based integration 
  2. Boomi custom integration to SAP IS a Pre-Packaged integration 
  3. Boomi Custom Integration to SF Integration Center Integrations
  4. Custom Boomi to Custom SAP Integration Suite 

5.Interface Prioritization

  1. Prioritize high-risk integrations such as Payroll, Time, and Benefits 
  2. Ensure SAP IS generates output with the same logic as Boomi.
  3. Validate output files between Boomi and SAP IS to streamline testing.

entry.title: Smarter HR with Joule: What's New in SAP SuccessFactors – 1H 2025 Release
entry.content:
SAP’s AI copilot, Joule, has had a major upgrade in the 1H 2025 release across SAP SuccessFactors modules, including Employee Central, Recruiting, Learning, and Onboarding. Here’s a concise summary of what’s new and real screenshots from SAP documentation.

1. Joule Now Available on Mobile

SAP has officially rolled out Joule in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app. Users can perform HR tasks like requesting time off or updating contact info using conversational prompts directly on their phones.

 

2. New Use Cases for Position Management (March 2025)

Two new transactional use cases are now supported:

  • Change "To Be Hired" Status of Position
  • Change Working Time for Position

3. Full Upgrade to New Conversational Experience (January 2025)

All Joule use cases now follow a simplified, intuitive chat-based interface:

  • New: Create Time Off (Transactional)
  • Upgraded: Create Positions, Edit Contact Info, Job Changes, Promotions, Transfers & more

4. Enhancements to Employee Central Quick Actions

  • Attachments now supported for key events (e.g., Promotion, Location Change)
  • New templates for One-Time Payment and Marital Status Change
  • Phone number formatting improvements

 

5. Expanded Learning & Performance Use Cases

Users can now:

  • Approve training requests (internal, external, with/without e-sign)
  • Manage and edit performance goals, add comments, assign buddies
  • View and comment on development goals

6. Improved Recruiting, Platform, and Succession Integration

Joule now supports:

  • Approving job requisitions and offers
  • Viewing job profiles, mentoring requests, and succession nominations

7. Config & Permission Requirements

To activate new features:

  • Joule must be enabled in the backend
  • Users must have “Access to Joule” permission under General User Permissions
  • Templates and Data Models must be updated for attachments

Summary

The 1H 2025 release of Joule marks a huge leap in conversational AI-driven HR transformation. From mobile access and deeper integration across modules to intuitive self-services and actionable insights, Joule empowers both employees and managers like never before.

#SAPJoule #SAPSuccessFactors #JouleUseCases #HXMExperience #DigitalHR #AIinHR #SAPCommunity

entry.title: A quick introduction to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Diagnostic Tool
entry.content:
What is the SAP SuccessFactors Diagnostics Tool?

The Employee Central Diagnostic Tool is a self-service feature that helps HR Administrators, Implementation Consultants and Customer Support teams identify and resolve system issues quickly. Whether it's a failed workflow, a misfiring rule, or a configuration inconsistency, the tool offers visibility into what's happening behind the scenes - without needing to comb through backend logs.

Key Benefits

  • Workflow & Rule Tracing: Understand exactly which rules and workflows triggered during transactions like New Hire or ESS/MSS events.
  • Centralized Services Transactions (Employee Data Save Transactions): Trace how data changes are processed and spot issues in real-time. And how save rules are executed in detail and sequence.
  • Proactive Troubleshooting: Catch misconfigurations early and reduce dependency on external support.

How It Works

This tool is built leveraging the Centralized Services Feature which was incrementally delivered for all EC entities in last 3 years.

When Should You Use It?

Use the Diagnostic Tool when:

  • A workflow fails to trigger, or routes incorrectly.
  • Business rules don't behave as expected during a hire, transfer, or data change.
  • You want to validate configuration changes in a test environment.
  • You're preparing logs and insights for a support ticket.
  • You need to know what the reason for a field value change was when you actually changed another field.
  • You want to proactively audit rule execution and system behavior.

Want to try it?

  1. Head to Admin Center > Diagnostic Tool in your SAP SuccessFactors environment, choose the type of transaction you with to analyze (e.g. Centralized Services or Workflows).

     

  2. Enable tracing using the link in landing page if you want to capture save transactions. For workflows trace capturing this enablement is not required.

     

  3. Next, reproduce the issue by saving changes in Employee's Data- any HRIS/EC entity like Job, personal, compensation information with right Role-based permissions enabled for you.
  4. Go back to the Diagnostic Tool and search for your recent transaction by entering 'Requested By' and today's date.
  5. Select the transaction you want to analyze and review Snapshot view for field changes or Rule Execution View to analyze rule executions during the transaction.

     

Final Thoughts

This tool puts diagnostic power directly in your hands - making it easier to resolve issues faster, ensure compliance, and improve user experience.

Resources:

Here are Official SAP Resources:

SAP Help Portal - Diagnostic Tool

What's New Viewer - 1H 2024 - New Diagnostic Tool for Employee Central Transactions

What's New Viewer - 2H 2024 - New View and Enhancements to the Diagnostic Tool

What's New Viewer - 1H 2025 - Enhancements to the Diagnostic Tool

Knowledge Base Articles:

 3468733 - Diagnostic Tool for Employee Central save transactions and workflows

3469437 - Incorrect email notification, workflow approvers, business rule for workflow or workflow configuration is selected by system - Employee Central
3469432 - Centralized Services save actions are resulting in incorrect data update - Employee Central

Next Steps:

Looking forward for you to use this tool and try if it helps in your configurations/issue analysis, share your feedback how useful it was for you, and also if anything else you would like to see in this tool with me!

You can leverage the comment section here or formally create SAP Customer Influence idea.

2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products

entry.title: SuccessFactors Testing - UiPath Best Practices
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors, a leading cloud-based HCM solution, plays a critical role in HR transformation across enterprises. However, its dynamic UI and frequent quarterly updates pose significant challenges to testing teams.

In this article, we’ll explore how UiPath’s orchestrated execution architecture along with five technical best practices to ensure scalable, maintainable, and resilient test automation in Success Factors projects.

🔧Architecture Components

  • UiPath Test Manager: Central test lifecycle management, AI powered test design, integrated with requirements (e.g., Azure DevOps, Jira) and defect tracking.
  • UiPath Studio: Design environment for modular, reusable test assets.
  • UiPath Orchestrator: Manages test sets, orchestrated execution management, environment provisioning, and parallel execution.
  • UiPath Robots: Responsible for test execution (on-prem or cloud VMs), triggered by Orchestrator.
  • Test Data Fabric (TDM): Connects, Stores and manages test data from any data source. Reusable across test cases.
  • SuccessFactors Test System: Pre-prod or sandbox tenant connected via secured web endpoints.

 🧭 Orchestrated Execution Flow:

  • Tests orchestrated from Test Manager, Orchestrator, or pipeline (CI/CD integration) based on events, schedules, and data availability.
  • Orchestrator Queues and Allocates test set to available robots.
  • Robots Authenticate to SuccessFactors via SSO/SAML or OAuth.
  • Test Data Retrieved dynamically from Data Fabric, external sources, or external API.
  • Tests Executed across modules (e.g., Employee Central, Recruiting, Learning).
  • Results Published to Test Manager, notified the key stakeholders, and synced to defect system if failures detected.

☁️Integrate with SAP Cloud ALM or Solution Manager for end-to-end visibility.

 Technical Best Practices

1. 🎯 Object Class-Specific Activities for Smart Controls

SuccessFactors UI relies heavily on Smart List Boxes, Dynamic Picklists, and Custom MDF Objects. These controls often require more than a simple click:

  • Build or reuse custom activities for these complex controls (e.g., 2-step selection for Smart List Boxes).
  • Leverage Unified Object Model framework with strict anchoring and fuzziness controls to improve selector reliability.

2. 🧱 Modular Test Design with Data Abstraction

Design test cases as modular workflows, separated by business processes:

  • Use Library Projects for reusable components like Login, Hire Employee, Terminate Employee.
  • Apply data-driven testing with Test Data Queues or TDS to separate test logic from test inputs.
  • Parameterize selectors where possible to support cross-tenant testing.

3. 🔁 Continuous Validation Against Quarterly Releases

SuccessFactors releases quarterly updates that often impact UI layouts or backend APIs.

  • Automate pre-release smoke tests in preview tenants using a scheduled execution plan.
  • Use dynamic selector validation to highlight potentially breaking changes early.
  • Maintain a centralized impact map of SuccessFactors module updates and map it to affected test cases in Test Manager.
  • Utilize SAP release calendar for Success Factors for Test planning: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/successfactors/product-release-road-map

4. ⚙️ Hybrid Testing Strategy: UI + API

Combine UI testing with OData API testing for robust validations:

  • Fetch test data (e.g., employee ID, job history) via API and validate it against UI results.
  • Automate backend data consistency checks post-action (e.g., after termination, verify status via OData).
  • Utilize pre-built API activities available for Success Factors

5. 📊 Integrate Test Insights with Reporting Dashboards

Use Test Manager analytics, Insights Dashboards to publish custom dashboards

  • Track metrics like pass rate, execution duration, failed test trend, and coverage by module.
  • Integrate with tools like Power BI, Grafana, or ElasticSearch for executive visibility.

Conclusion:

With quarterly updates, changing UIs, and connected modules, testing SuccessFactors can be challenging. By using UiPath and following key best practices highlighted above, teams can scale testing across modules, boost release confidence, cut down effort, and improve audit readiness in Success Factors projects.

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Up Joule with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
SAP is at the forefront of AI innovations, with Joule - SAP's AI assistant - revolutionizing the management of HR tasks within SuccessFactors. Joule provides intelligent support for HR processes, facilitating quick answers, task automation, and streamlined decision-making.

This blog presents a practical guide on deploying Joule in SAP SuccessFactors. The instructions are clear and accessible for both functional and technical teams.

Before You Begin Ensure that you have:

  • An active SAP SuccessFactors License that includes access to SAP Joule.
  • A global account in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
  • Successfully integrated SuccessFactors with SAP Cloud Identity Services for secure user logins.

Step 1: Establish Trust Between SAP BTP and Identity Authentication This setup allows Joule to authenticate users through Identity Authentication.

  • Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit.
  • Go to Security → Trust Configuration.
  • Establish Trust by adding your IAS tenant as a trusted identity provider.

Step 2: Configure User Attributes in IAS Joule requires basic user details like first name, last name, and email for personalization.

  • Navigate to the IAS Administration Console.
  • Select User Management → User Attributes.
  • Define and map attributes such as email, firstName, and lastName.

Step 3: Launch Joule Booster in SAP BTP Use SAP's automated booster to simplify Joule configuration.

  • Access the SAP BTP Cockpit with global admin privileges.
  • Click on Boosters.
  • Locate and start the "Setting up Joule" booster.
  • Follow the steps to choose the subaccount and review entitlements.

Step 4: Add Trusted Domains in IAS for SuccessFactors Enable secure communication between SuccessFactors and Joule.

  • In the IAS Admin Console, go to Applications & Resources → Applications.
  • Select the SAP SuccessFactors application.
  • In the Trust tab, add the domain utilized by your SuccessFactors system.

Step 5: Assign Joule Access via Permission Roles in SuccessFactors Grant relevant permissions to users for Joule access.

  • Log in to SuccessFactors and navigate to Admin Center.
  • Manage Permission Roles.
  • Choose roles requiring Joule access.
  • Activate Joule Access checkbox under User Permissions.

Step 6: Test and Confirm Integration Ensure Joule functions correctly post-setup.

Test Procedures:

  • Log in to SAP SuccessFactors with appropriate permissions.
  • Locate the Joule icon or chat assistant.
  • Engage with Joule by inputting queries to validate responses.

In Conclusion By adhering to these steps, you can seamlessly integrate Joule into SAP SuccessFactors, enhancing HR workflows with AI capabilities. This integration amplifies user engagement and efficiency through intelligent insights and automation, whether for full deployment or a pilot group.

Review the official SAP docs here.

Joule

entry.title: How to pull data from the 'Login User' record and populate a custom field.
entry.content:
When you manually start the onboarding process there is no quick and easy way of seeing who ‘initiated’ the process.  Here is a small custom solution to help alleviate this. 

Create a business rule as follows.  We are going to build this rule to populate a custom field in employment information called ‘Recruiters Name’.  At first we added in the ‘Then’ statement = login user, but this just populates the person-id-external of the login user.  However, if you add in ‘login user’ as a variable, this will allow you to access the login user data via the ‘then' statement.

Result

This solution can be used to extract data from the login user’s profile in Onboarding or Employee Central
